metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students develop photography skills including camera operation, composition, and image editing. This course emphasizes visual storytelling, technical control, and personal artistic vision in photographic practice.
metadata.general_description: A course focused on photography as an art form, camera operation, composition, and image editing.

big_ideas.statement: Photography is a medium for artistic expression, documentation, and visual communication.
big_ideas.elaboration: Photographers use cameras and image technology to create artistic work, document reality, and communicate messages and perspectives to audiences.

big_ideas.statement: Camera operation and technical control enable photographers to realize their artistic vision.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding exposure, focus, composition, and camera settings allows photographers to intentionally create images that match their creative intentions.

big_ideas.statement: Composition and visual literacy are essential to creating compelling photographic images.
big_ideas.elaboration: Strong composition, use of light, and visual organization guide viewers' perception and create engaging, meaningful photographs.

big_ideas.statement: Digital editing extends photographers' creative possibilities and enables refinement of their work.
big_ideas.elaboration: Post-processing tools allow photographers to enhance, adjust, and transform images to achieve their artistic vision.

competencies.raw: Develop camera operation and technical skills
i_can_statements: I can operate a camera and adjust exposure settings manually
I can understand and control aperture, shutter speed, and ISO
I can focus accurately and select appropriate focus modes
I can take technically sound photographs with intentional control
I can compose photographs using visual principles
I can frame subjects effectively and use space intentionally
I can use leading lines, depth, and other compositional techniques
I can create visually compelling and balanced photographs

competencies.raw: Develop photographic projects and bodies of work
i_can_statements: I can plan and execute photography projects
I can develop a consistent photographic subject or theme
I can create series of related images
I can build coherent bodies of photographic work

competencies.raw: Edit and enhance photographs using digital tools
i_can_statements: I can use photo editing software to refine images
I can adjust exposure, contrast, and color
I can crop and compose images in post-processing
I can enhance and transform photographs artistically
I can identify compositional and technical choices in photographs
I can explain how photographic choices affect viewer perception
I can analyze light, subject, and framing in images
I can evaluate the effectiveness of photographic work

competencies.raw: Understand photography as art and communication
i_can_statements: I can understand photography's role as an art form
I can recognize photography's use in documentary and artistic contexts
I can analyze how photographs communicate meaning
I can understand ethical considerations in photographic practice

competencies.raw: Reflect on personal photographic practice and vision
i_can_statements: I can evaluate my photographic work and artistic choices
I can identify my photographic interests and style
I can assess my technical and artistic growth
I can set goals for developing as a photographer

competencies.raw: Examine photography in cultural and historical contexts
i_can_statements: I can understand photography's history and evolution
I can analyze photographs as cultural documents
I can recognize diverse photographic traditions
I can understand representation and perspective in photography

competencies.raw: Organize and manage photographic files and projects
i_can_statements: I can organize and backup digital image files
I can maintain metadata and organization systems
I can manage large bodies of photographic work
I can preserve and archive photographs professionally
I can display photographs effectively in exhibitions or presentations
I can select and edit images for presentation
I can create photo books or portfolios
I can present work to audiences with confidence

competencies.raw: Communicate through photography and visual narrative
i_can_statements: I can create photographs that tell stories or convey meaning
I can use photography to communicate personal perspectives
I can create photo essays or narrative sequences
I can engage viewers through my photographic work

competencies.raw: Share photography online and through digital platforms
i_can_statements: I can share photographs on social media and online platforms
I can optimize images for different digital contexts
I can build an online photographic presence
I can engage audiences through digital photography sharing

competencies.raw: Connect photography to other visual arts and media forms
i_can_statements: I can understand photography's relationship to visual art
I can integrate photography with other media
I can use photography in mixed media work
I can explore photography across multiple contexts

competencies.raw: Explore diverse photographic approaches and genres
i_can_statements: I can experiment with different photographic genres
I can study diverse photographic traditions
I can adapt techniques from varied photographic practices
I can develop personal photographic style

competencies.raw: Engage with photography as social and cultural commentary
i_can_statements: I can create photographs exploring social or cultural themes
I can use photography to document and interpret reality
I can address meaningful issues through photographic work
I can contribute to conversations through photography

competencies.raw: Build awareness of photography careers and professional practices
i_can_statements: I can understand diverse photography careers and specializations
I can develop a professional portfolio of photographic work
I can explore pathways to professional photography
I can understand professional photography standards and ethics

areas.name: Camera Operation and Technical Fundamentals
topics: Exposure triangle: aperture, shutter speed, ISO

areas.name: Composition and Visual Storytelling
topics: Rule of thirds and leading lines

areas.name: Photographic Practice and Projects
topics: Photographic series and bodies of work
